title: Parkio Daily for July 20, 2026
teaser: Monstropolis construction accelerates at Hollywood Studios; Der Teddybar reopens at EPCOT after six years; Tower of Terror runs limited shafts.

bignews.title: Monstropolis entrance takes shape at Hollywood Studios
bignews.body: Aerial photos show the first themed concrete installed at the future Monstropolis entrance, with decorative columns and trim work now visible. The land transformation continues as construction crews shape the gateway between Animation Courtyard and the new attractions.

parkioInsight.text: Construction noise and fencing around Animation Courtyard tends to make navigation tight during peak afternoon hours. Plan your route through Toy Story Land or Galaxy's Edge when heading to Tower of Terror or Rock 'n' Roller Coaster.

bignews.title: Der Teddybar reopens in Germany pavilion after six years
bignews.body: The plush and toy shop in EPCOT's Germany pavilion has reopened following a closure that began in 2020. The store offers German-themed merchandise and stuffed animals in World Showcase.

parkioInsight.text: Newly reopened shops often see a surge of curious visitors in the first few weeks. Stop by early morning or late evening to browse without shoulder-to-shoulder crowds.

bignews.title: Tower of Terror operating with reduced capacity
bignews.body: The Twilight Zone Tower of Terror is running fewer elevator shafts following repeated mechanical issues, resulting in longer-than-usual queues. Wait times have climbed as Disney limits operational vehicles on the drop tower.

parkioInsight.text: Tower of Terror waits tend to peak between 11 a.m. and 3 p.m. Rope drop or the final hour before park close usually offer the shortest queues, even with limited shafts.

topstories.title: Magic Kingdom extends early entry for party season
topstories.body: Extra morning hours will return to Magic Kingdom during the 2026 fall party season, giving resort guests additional time before ticketed event closures. The extensions offset shorter operating days when after-hours events begin.

parkioInsight.text: Party nights typically push day guests out by 6 p.m. Resort guests can stack early entry with a park-hop to maximize attraction hours without the evening rush.

topstories.title: Descendants costumes and props now on display at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: Walt Disney Presents is showcasing costumes and props from Descendants: Wicked Wonderland, including Queen of Hearts outfits and set pieces. The exhibit joins other film and television memorabilia at the Animation Courtyard walkthrough.

parkioInsight.text: Walt Disney Presents rarely draws long lines. Duck in during midday heat or heavy rain when outdoor queues spike elsewhere in the park.

topstories.title: Coronado Springs Resort construction scheduled through 2027
topstories.body: Disney has calendared a year-plus renovation project at Coronado Springs Resort starting soon. The work appears focused on the convention center rather than guest rooms, though disruption details remain limited.

parkioInsight.text: Convention center projects can bring noise, fencing, and truck traffic to resort walkways. Guests who value quiet mornings may prefer Pop Century or Art of Animation until completion.

topstories.title: Nine Disney World restaurants update menus this week
topstories.body: Menu changes rolled out across multiple Walt Disney World dining locations this week, adjusting items and pricing at quick-service and table-service venues. Specific changes vary by restaurant.

parkioInsight.text: Menu updates often bring limited-time offerings that disappear within weeks. Check your reservation's online menu the morning of to spot new dishes worth trying.

topstories.title: Disney transportation suspensions announced
topstories.body: Disney Skyliner, boat, and bus service will see periodic suspensions for maintenance and weather. Guests should plan extra travel time and monitor notifications through My Disney Experience.

parkioInsight.text: Skyliner downtime tends to ripple into longer bus waits at Caribbean Beach and Riviera. Build a 20-minute buffer if you're hopping parks or heading to a dining reservation.

topstories.title: New Disney Animation experience coming to Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: An immersive Disney Animation-inspired attraction is planned for Hollywood Studios, designed to bring animated characters into a walk-through or interactive environment. Details on opening timeline and location remain under wraps.

parkioInsight.text: New Animation Courtyard offerings usually draw families with younger children. Soft openings often happen weeks ahead of formal announcements—watch for signage near Star Wars Launch Bay.

icymi.title: D23 Day at Disneyland set for August 13
icymi.body: D23 members can join a special event day at Disneyland on August 13, featuring exclusive entertainment and activities ahead of the D23 fan convention in Anaheim. Tickets and registration details are available through the D23 website.

icymi.title: Disney Jr. Let's Play Party returns to California Adventure
icymi.body: The Disney Jr.-themed event is scheduled for August 13 at Disney California Adventure, with character meet-and-greets and play zones for preschool guests. The celebration continues into the following days.

icymi.title: Very Merrytime Christmas cruises announced for late 2026
icymi.body: Disney Cruise Line will offer holiday-themed sailings in November and December 2026 with seasonal decorations, character appearances, and festive dining. Bookings are open for select itineraries.

icymi.title: Beach-themed ornaments arrive at Disneyland
icymi.body: Christmas in July merchandise includes beach-themed holiday ornaments featuring Stitch, Finding Nemo characters, and Mickey designs. The items are available now at Disneyland Resort shops.

spotlight.title: EPCOT World Showcase food tour under $100
spotlight.body: A new video walks through eating around World Showcase for less than one hundred dollars, highlighting fresh menu items and budget-friendly snacks at pavilions from Mexico to Canada. The route covers shareable plates and drinks without table-service reservations.

parkioInsight.text: World Showcase kiosks and quick-service windows tend to have shorter lines before 11 a.m. and after 7 p.m. Start your food crawl early or late to skip festival-sized crowds at each pavilion.
